name: range-why-generalists-triumph-in-a-specialized-world
description: David Epstein's Range: Why Generalists Triumph in a Specialized World — a cognitive science and career development toolkit challenging the cult of early specialization and the 10,000-hour rule, showing that in complex, unpredictable domains, generalists with broad experience consistently outperform narrow specialists.
useCase: Understand why breadth beats depth in complex domains — learn how to develop range through broad sampling, desirable difficulties, analogical thinking, and knowing when to quit and when to persist.
knownRisks: The generalist advantage is domain-dependent — in kind learning environments like surgery or competitive chess, specialization still matters
The book's thesis challenges deeply ingrained cultural beliefs about early specialization, which may feel counterintuitive
Young professionals might use 'exploration' as an excuse to avoid commitment — the key is productive sampling, not aimless drifting
